Polymarket Edge Lab

A read-only research harness validating the prediction-market favorite–longshot bias before a single euro is risked — the route chosen by your LLM Council for a €1,000 + €200/month account under a Polish tax standpoint.

Statistical power — what this test can and cannot see

The pre-registered PASS needs the 95% confidence interval's lower bound on the holdout longshot band to clear the 0.02 cost line. Profit per share in the band swings between ≈+0.10 (longshot fails) and ≈−0.90 (longshot hits), so the per-share standard deviation is ≈0.30 and the CI shrinks only with √n of the band sample — not the full usable sample.

  • Smallest gross edge detectable at the readout gate (Aug 19, holdout band n≈75): ≈8.8pp per share.
  • Smallest gross edge detectable at the hard stop (Sep 30, holdout band n≈141): ≈6.9pp per share.
  • The favorite–longshot bias documented in the literature for this price range is typically 2–5pp gross — smaller than either detection floor.

Honest implication: unless the Polymarket bias is unusually large, the likely readout is a correct "not detectable at this sample size" — a valid, pre-registered null, not a failure. This was a property of the frozen design from day one; per KILL_CRITERIA.md nothing gets changed mid-flight, and these numbers are informational only.

⚠ Critical constraint

You cannot legally open positions on Polymarket from Poland

Polymarket lists Poland as close-only (view & close existing positions, but new orders are rejected — gambling-law compliance). Kalshi is US-persons-only. So this project is strictly a research / edge-validation effort: it uses only public market data and never places an order. Do not use a VPN to evade the geoblock (it breaches Polymarket's terms and Polish law). This status could change under evolving MiCA enforcement — re-verify before any execution work.

Project timeline

  • Step 0 — Legal / tax gate

    RESOLVED

    Verified against Polymarket primary sources: Poland is close-only (cannot open positions). Kalshi is US-only. Execution from Poland is legally blocked; the read-only research harness proceeds.

  • Step 1 — Pre-registration

    FROZEN

    Causal mechanism, falsifiable hypothesis, buckets, snapshot horizon, liquidity filter and cost model locked in config.py before any resolved-outcome data existed.

  • Steps 2–4 — Build & schedule

    LIVE

    Logger, resolver and analyzer built and tested against the live Gamma API. Windows Task Scheduler runs the logger, resolver and dashboard hourly (:08/:12/:18); pipeline hardened 2026-07-03 (WAL, retries — see Lab notes).

  • Step 5 — Collect & validate

    IN PROGRESS

    Accumulating snapshots and resolutions for 6–8 weeks. Day 7 of ~49 · 319/200 usable resolved. The pre-registered read-out is the out-of-sample holdout, net of costs.

  • Step 6 — Decision gate

    BLOCKED (execution)

    If — and only if — a legal venue opens and the holdout edge survives net of costs, scope a separate, hard-capped €1,000 execution module. Until then this stays research; keep the €200/month in a low-cost index fund as the benchmark.

Current results

Pre-registered holdout verdict (longshot band 0.05–0.15)

Gross edge / share: +0.1046 (95% CI [+0.0904, +0.1188], n=13)
Cost model: −0.0200 · NET edge / share: +0.0846
Edge survives net of costs (CI lower bound > cost): YES

Full-sample calibration

Implied bucketNMean impliedRealizedGapWilson 95%

0.10-0.15120.1240.000+0.124[0.000, 0.243]
0.15-0.2070.1760.143+0.034[0.026, 0.513]
0.20-0.30280.2490.321-0.072[0.179, 0.507]

0.95-1.0030.9621.000-0.038[0.438, 1.000]

A positive gap (mean implied > realized) in low buckets = longshots overpriced = the edge under test. The gap equals the expected profit per share of betting against that outcome.

What the council decided (2026-07-02, project charter)

Where the council agreed

  • At €1k–3.4k, absolute profit rounds to zero — the deliverable is a validated edge + reusable infrastructure, not euros.
  • Don't let the installed Solana/memecoin toolkit pick the strategy — that's sunk-cost reasoning with the worst fixed-cost ratio.
  • Validate the edge before building execution or risking a cent.
  • The prediction-market favorite–longshot bias is the one route with a named mechanism and small enough universe to avoid p-hacking.

Where it clashed & what it caught

  • Tax: asset or trap? Deferral is not alpha, and crypto losses ring-fence to crypto gains only. Best read: an iteration advantage, not a profit one.
  • Build vs don't: two advisors argued an index fund beats the bots — cap this at hobby/tuition risk.
  • Blind spot (all 3 reviewers): legal access. This is the gate that turned out to block execution from Poland.
  • Nobody had verified the longshot edge survives fees/competition — which is exactly what this lab now measures.

The recommendation & the one thing

Recommendation: Treat this as an edge-validation project. Build the read-only logger, run the pre-registered out-of-sample test over 6–8 weeks, and only ever consider execution if the edge survives net of costs and a legal venue exists. Success = "a documented edge survived an honest test," not profit.

The one thing: answer the two kill-switch questions before funding anything — can a Polish resident legally trade it, and how is it taxed? Done — and it revealed execution is blocked, saving you from building a dead-end.

Latest check-in: council #3 (2026-07-03) reviewed the strategy against live data and ruled stay the course — no new tracks; full verdict on the Lab notes tab.

Owner's guide

How the machine works

logger.py (hourly) → snapshots the implied probability of the top-liquidity two-outcome sports markets from Polymarket's public Gamma API into SQLite. No account, no wallet, no trading. resolve.py (hourly) → records the YES/NO outcome of markets that have resolved. analyze.py → buckets markets by implied probability at T−24h and compares each bucket's price to how often it actually came true, on an untouched out-of-sample holdout, net of a cost model. dashboard.py → regenerates this page hourly.

Run anything by hand

python logger.py · python resolve.py · python analyze.py · python analyze.py --selftest (verifies the math on synthetic injected-bias data) · python dashboard.py

How to read the result

The gap = mean implied probability − realized rate in a bucket. In the low (longshot) buckets, a persistent positive gap on the holdout, larger than the cost model, means longshots are systematically overpriced and betting against them is +EV. If the holdout gap is ~zero or the confidence interval crosses the cost line, there is no tradeable edge — the honest and most common outcome, and a valid, valuable answer.

Stop / go thresholds (pre-committed)

  • Continue only if: the holdout edge stays positive net of costs across ≥200 resolved markets.
  • Scale only if: it also holds across two distinct market regimes — and a legal venue exists.
  • Stop if: the edge decays out-of-sample, or the CI crosses the cost line. Decay is the expected fate of most published patterns.

Learn the concepts

Favorite–longshot bias

Across racetrack, sports and prediction markets, bettors systematically overpay for unlikely "exciting" outcomes (longshots) and underprice near-certain favorites. The counterparty losing money is the recreational/partisan bettor. This lab measures whether it is large enough on Polymarket to beat costs.

Implied probability & the "reference outcome"

A share price in [0,1] reads directly as a probability (0.08 = 8%). Each market has two outcomes; we fix the first-listed one as the reference and track its price and whether it came true — this works for Yes/No, team-vs-team and Over/Under alike.

Out-of-sample / holdout

Markets are split by resolution time; the second half is never used for tuning. An edge only counts if it appears on that untouched holdout — this is what separates a real edge from a curve-fit.

Wilson interval & cost model

The Wilson 95% interval is an honest error bar on a win-rate from a finite sample. The cost model subtracts spread/slippage (2%) + gas before any edge is declared real, because at €1k fixed costs dominate.

Polish tax facts (for when/if a legal venue exists)

Flat 19% (PIT-38); crypto-to-crypto swaps tax-neutral until fiat conversion; crypto ring-fenced as capital income (no ZUS, no business reclassification); 5-year loss carryforward — but crypto losses offset only future crypto gains. Prediction-market P&L classification (crypto vs gambling) is unconfirmed and would need an individual ruling (ORD-IN).

The base-rate reality

97% of persistent futures day traders lost money; <1% of day traders are reliably profitable. This is why the project is framed as skill + infrastructure with capped tuition, not a profit centre.

Next steps

  • Let the scheduled logger/resolver run — the clock to the Aug 19 readout gate is the binding constraint.
  • Check this dashboard weekly (Health tab first); watch the longshot gap and whether the holdout CI clears the cost line — informational only until readout.
  • Optional 5-minute act sanctioned by council #3: git init + a dated HOLDOUT_BOUNDARY.md ("data after this timestamp is holdout for any future test").
  • At the readout gate (2026-08-19), run the pre-registered holdout verdict once and write it up — the structural findings lead, whatever the verdict (see Lab notes).
  • If pursuing further, get a Polish individual tax ruling (ORD-IN) on prediction-market P&L classification.
  • Keep the €200/month in a low-cost index fund meanwhile — the council's honest benchmark.

If Polymarket were legal — hypothetical paper P&L

⚠ Simulation only — no orders are or can be placed

Execution from Poland remains blocked (close-only). This page re-prices the pre-registered strategy on the same public data the test collects: bet against every longshot in the frozen 0.05–0.15 band at its T−24h snapshot, settle at event time, frozen cost model applied. It is an illustration of scale, not part of the pre-registered test and not evidence the edge is real — only the holdout verdict at n≥200 decides that.

Paper bankroll
€218.17
started €200.00 · Jul 01, 11:53 UTC
Total P&L
+€18.17
+9.1% over 7.2 days
Avg monthly return
+44.4%
extrapolated from 7.2 days — see caveats
Trades settled
19
19 won · 0 lost
Trade rate
≈80/mo
at current band flow

Paper equity curve — €200.00 start, all settled band trades

Steps mark trade settlements; the line holds flat between events. Hover or focus a dot for the trade readout. Dot = one settled trade.

Simulation rules

  • Signal: the exact markets the pre-registered test uses — implied prob in [0.05, 0.15) at the snapshot nearest T−24h (in-sample + holdout combined).
  • Trade: buy the opposite side at 1−p; it pays €1/share if the longshot fails, €0 if it hits (stake is fully lost).
  • Sizing: fixed fraction — 5% of the running bankroll per trade, compounding in settlement order.
  • Costs (frozen in config.py): 0.02/share spread+slippage and $0.05 gas per trade.
  • Simplifications: EUR = USDC 1:1 (no FX), fills assumed at snapshot price, no deposit/withdrawal costs, no overlapping-position cash constraint.

Why you cannot bank the number above

  • All 19 trades won so far — but even if prices were perfectly calibrated (zero edge), a run this clean happens 12% of the time. Nothing is proven yet.
  • A losing trade forfeits its whole stake (5% of bankroll). At these prices expect roughly 1 loss per 10 trades — none has occurred yet, which flatters the extrapolation.
  • The monthly figure annualizes 7.2 days and 19 trades. It is statistically meaningless until the pre-registered sample (n≥200) is in — readout per KILL_CRITERIA.md.
  • If markets are calibrated (no edge), costs alone grind this strategy to ≈−10.4%/mo — the null you are betting against.
  • Council reality check: even with a real edge and venue access, realistic capacity at this account size is ≈€50–300/yr, not a compounding money machine.

Pipeline health — live

Everything below is recomputed from the database and the job logs each time this page regenerates. Green = the experiment is collecting cleanly; anything red deserves a look at the named log file.

logger runs hourly at :08

Database

journal mode: wal

Snapshot gaps

Last good hour before gapHours missing
2026-07-07 04:00 UTC6h
2026-07-07 18:00 UTC4h
2026-07-07 23:00 UTC3h
2026-07-08 03:00 UTC2h
2026-07-08 06:00 UTC2h
2026-07-08 09:00 UTC1h
2026-07-08 11:00 UTC2h
2026-07-08 14:00 UTC1h

Known causes of gaps: the machine asleep at task time (Task Scheduler fires a catch-up run on wake), transient DNS/network failures (now retried ×3 with backoff), and one historical database is locked collision (fixed 2026-07-03 with WAL + busy_timeout). Each missing hour slightly thins the usable sample but does not bias it — outages are independent of match outcomes.

logger.log — snapshot collection

Last entry—
Error lines (last 400)0

Healthy = one “scanned_liquid … snapshots_written” line per hour. Transient network errors are retried ×3 before a run gives up.

resolve.log — outcome labeling

Last entry—
Error lines (last 400)0

Healthy = one “pending_checked … newly_resolved” line per hour. newly_resolved=0 is normal when no tracked game has finished since the last run.

Usable-market funnel — why 319 of 1360 resolved markets count

CategoryN%Meaning
Usable ✓31923%has a liquid snapshot within ±12h of T−24h — enters the pre-registered test
Pre-coverage473%its T−24h window closed before collection began on Jul 01 — startup transient, stops growing
Entered market late83762%first appeared in the top-liquidity universe after its T−24h window — structural, see below
Other15712%sparse/illiquid snapshots around the target window

The structural finding: half of all resolved sports markets are first seen 4.6h before the game (quartiles: -2.5h to +15.6h relative to game start; negative = seen only in-game). Sports markets only become liquid on game day, so a T−24h test can only ever see the subset already liquid a day ahead — which is also the only subset that would have been tradeable at T−24h. The low usable yield is a property of the market, not a bug.

How this page keeps itself up to date

  • Data: logger (hourly :08) and resolver (hourly :12) refresh edge_lab.sqlite; every number on every tab is recomputed from that file when the dashboard regenerates (hourly :18).
  • Page: auto-reloads every 15 minutes (meta refresh), so the browser tab never shows a render older than ~75 minutes.
  • Self-diagnosis: the "Updated" badge in the header turns red if the page it is showing was generated >2h ago — that means the Dashboard task itself died.
  • Dead-man's switch: watchdog.py (daily 10:00) pops a Windows alert if snapshots are >6h stale or the resolver has not run for >3h.

Lab notes — decisions & changes on the record

This tab is the project's paper trail: what was changed, what was found, and what was decided — each entry dated. The live numbers these notes refer to are always current on the Overview and Health tabs; full detail lives in REVIEW-2026-07-03.md in the project folder.

2026-07-03 — Pipeline hardening (maintenance, frozen parameters untouched)

A code review found three data-loss failure modes already visible in the logs. Fixes, all verified with a selftest + live smoke runs (backup of pre-change code and DB in backup-2026-07-03/):

Problem observed Fix applied
“database is locked” abort when jobs collided after the machine woke from sleep (all catch-up tasks fire at once) SQLite WAL journal + 15s busy_timeout; network fetching moved outside write transactions in logger & resolver
5 hourly runs lost to single transient DNS failures HTTP retry ×3 with 2s/4s backoff on DNS/timeouts/5xx/429
Resolver re-fetched voided/ambiguous markets forever (unbounded run growth) Resolution queue bounded at 14 days past the event
28h outcome-labeling stall on Jul 2–3 Root cause was the resolver being scheduled daily while everything assumed hourly — schedule fixed, docstring corrected
Naive timestamps parsed in local time (latent) Timezone-less values now assume UTC

Known-and-accepted (not fixed, by choice, to respect the feature freeze): this dashboard reloads the full snapshot table each render (fine for the 12-week project life); the watchdog detects “resolver not running”, not “resolver failing”; no git history (a one-time git init is recommended).

2026-07-03 — Council check-in #3: “is there a better strategy?” — verdict: stay the course

Unanimous

  • No news-research / event-prediction track: ~100 honest, non-cherry-picked events at 30–60 min each is a ~40× overrun of the 15 min/week budget; hand-picked events are unfalsifiable; even a win is untradeable from Poland.
  • The frozen test stays frozen.
  • The structural findings (markets appear ~0.4h before game time; extreme-price markets are scarce at every horizon) are the real scientific payload of this project.

Decided on the numbers

  • A parallel “Track B” pre-registration (favorites band / shorter horizon) was seriously argued — and rejected because a power analysis on the live data showed every configuration is also underpowered before the Sep 30 hard stop (best case detects ≈6.4pp vs a 2–5pp literature effect). Registering it would pre-commit a second predictable null.
  • Only sanctioned act: a 5-minute dated holdout boundary note (“data after this timestamp is holdout for any future test”), preserving clean data for post-readout ideas.
  • Chairman's standing rule: that council was the one renegotiation this contract gets. Future urges to add tracks → reread that verdict, don't reconvene.

The termination contract (KILL_CRITERIA.md, pre-committed 2026-07-02) — what happens next, no matter what

DateEvent Action
Until readoutCollection continues unattendedZero new feature work · maintenance ≤15 min/week · analyzer output not acted on
2026-08-19Readout gate (if usable n ≥ 200 — already assured)Run the pre-registered holdout test once, write up 1–2 pages, then archive: PASS → writeup, still no trading while Poland is close-only · FAIL → equally publishable null writeup
2026-09-30Hard stop Readout happens with whatever sample exists; all four scheduled tasks disabled; repo + writeup kept as the portfolio artifact

Standing decisions independent of the verdict: capital plan unchanged (buffer → world ETF in IKE); no on-chain betting venues; no VPN/geoblock evasion, ever. If reading this creates an urge to renegotiate — that urge is the thing the contract was built to catch.

Champion odds — 10,000 bracket simulations

▲▼ change vs previous run. Resolved matches enter as certainties; unplayed slots are simulated from the model.

Track record — frozen at issue, graded after the whistle

MatchModel pick (P advance)ActualHitBrier
Round of 16 · Brazil v NorwayBrazil 70%Norway advanced (1-2)✗0.490
Round of 16 · Mexico v EnglandMexico 55%England advanced (2-3)✗0.302
Round of 16 · Portugal v SpainSpain 66%Spain advanced (0-1)✓0.114
Round of 16 · United States v BelgiumUnited States 52%Belgium advanced (1-4)✗0.271
Round of 16 · Argentina v EgyptArgentina 82%Argentina advanced (3-2)✓0.031

Brier = (P(advance) − outcome)². Predictions are never revised after issue. Blend sanity: pure-Elo (w=0) Brier 0.156, pure-tournament (w=1) 0.397, blend 0.242.

Toy Poisson model anchored on pre-tournament Elo (end-2025 snapshot), updated by tournament goals with hard shrinkage (~5 games/team). Extra time at λ/3, penalties a coin flip. Champion odds are ±10pt fuzzy. Not a betting signal — and execution is blocked from Poland anyway.
